semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all five exact source mappings, the schema-valid evaluation record, inherited RoPE receipt boundary, both public-safe Circle cyclic receipt slices, both no-change decisions, both proof targets, all seven CyclicMixers theorem declarations, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made the model-, layer-, mechanism-version-, workload-, baseline-, kernel-, hardware-, claim-axis-, and time-specific Cyclic Mechanism Tradeoff Packet the distinct owner; expanded it to eighteen mechanisms, twelve interfaces, eighteen invariants, and eighteen failure modes; preserved zero chapter-core effect; and froze the natural-workload, real-kernel, matched-baseline, causal, independent-reproduction, and heterogeneous-transfer campaign.

scope.population: Named CoilRA, MultiCoil, rotary, phase-bank, route-head, circulant, block-cyclic, or other cyclic mechanism candidates for declared layers, workloads, consumers, baselines, kernels, hardware, claim axes, budgets, rights, and horizons.
scope.environment: Five exact mappings; one schema record; one inherited RoPE structural boundary; two public-safe Circle cyclic receipt slices; two no-change decisions; two proof targets; seven finite declarations. No trained cyclic model, natural workload, full baseline matrix, real kernel or hardware result, independent reproduction, or transfer result is represented.
scope.model: A model-, layer-, mechanism-version-, workload-, baseline-, kernel-, hardware-, claim-axis-, environment-, budget-, and time-specific Cyclic Mechanism Tradeoff Packet over structural, numerical, empirical, resource, safety, rights, fallback, recovery, and residual evidence.
scope.authority: This chapter owns cyclic-specific diagnostics and empirical tradeoff interpretation only. Mathematical/Search owns general adoption; Proof Transport, Representation/Context, Routing, State-Carry, Training, Runtime, Resource Economics, Verification, Security/Privacy/Rights, Readiness/Incident/Release, and Claims/Evidence retain their declared boundaries.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-15; mechanism, model, layer, implementation, kernel, compiler, workload, baseline, hardware, policy, rights, threat, consumer, environment, or time changes expire affected packets.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest; five mappings; one schema record; RoPE structural boundary; cyclic-mixer and MultiCoil receipts; two no-change decisions; two targets; seven declarations; semantic review; and future natural real-kernel causal-transfer campaign.

assumptions: The exact mechanism, model, layer, implementation, kernel, workload, baselines, hardware, claim axes, rights, budgets, and expiry can be observed and versioned.

Dense, LoRA, ordinary RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, state-space, wrong-period, random-phase, nonperiodic, and no-mechanism controls can run with matched data, resources, tuning, engineering, and evaluator opportunity.
Real kernels and independent mechanism, kernel, and evaluator implementations can expose structural, numerical, useful-outcome, resource, safety, rights, fallback, recovery, and residual behavior.
Divergence, OOM, timeout, unsupported shape, missing kernel, fallback, repair, failed seed, and excluded cases remain in denominators.
Causal ablations, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer can be frozen prospectively.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary dense, LoRA, RoPE, learned-position, recurrent, and state-space mechanisms dominate; cyclic structure adds alias, numerical, kernel, and governance burden without a useful joint advantage.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Matched natural real-hardware campaigns show no unique joint benefit or reveal alias, numerical, quality, stability, kernel, cost, safety, rights, fallback, recovery, reproduction, or transfer failures; or weak baselines, outcome-driven search, hidden work, shared evaluators, or imported source results explain the apparent gain.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current cyclic, RoPE, low-rank, recurrent, state-space, and kernel comparators without importing reported results.
Justify workload-native cyclic structure, least authority, rights, canary scope, fallback, expiry, retirement, and residual ownership.
Specify mechanism semantics, finite-to-real refinement, numerical error, kernel-to-reference equivalence, countermodels, and fallback liveness beyond record projections.
Run actual cyclic mechanisms, real kernels, unsupported shapes, failures, fallback, recovery, and matched controls.
Measure structural, numerical, quality, context, stability, tail latency, throughput, complete resource, safety, rights, recovery, and residual outcomes jointly.
Ablate tying, phase, winding, block, circulant, relative rotation, and hardware specialization under matched opportunity.
Reproduce independently and transfer across models, scales, tasks, lengths, accelerators, compilers, organizations, attacks, and time.

contrary_evidence: The schema and imported public-safe receipts establish structural record boundaries only, not learned or deployed behavior.
Dense-reference parity is bounded to a fixture and is not model-quality, numerical-generalization, runtime, memory, or deployment evidence.
Finite phase and relative-shift facts do not establish usable context, attention quality, retrieval, stability, or transfer.
Seven declarations are two direct projections and five finite negative cases; none proves mechanism semantics, kernel behavior, useful outcomes, fallback liveness, reproduction, or transfer.
No chapter-core transition, natural workload campaign, real kernel benchmark, independent reproduction, or heterogeneous transfer exists.
known_confounds: Model, layer, data, task, length, period, rank, block size, precision, compiler, accelerator, batching, kernel, and tuning can dominate outcomes.
Synthetic fixtures and fixed receipts omit training dynamics, evolving workloads, failures, drift, and deployment state.
Shared mechanism code, kernel, evaluator, data, compiler defect, or benchmark flaw can create false agreement.
Averages can hide aliases, load tails, numerical collapse, long-length failure, subgroups, and silent slow paths.
Parameter or theoretical operation savings can disappear in optimizer state, activations, traffic, transforms, synchronization, fallback, and governance work.
acceptance_criteria: Every lifecycle stage has a producer, consumer, valid case, rejecting mutation, consequence, recovery route, and residual owner.
A real reference lane executes natural cyclic-mechanism work with full controls, kernels, failures, fallback, recovery, and complete metrics.
The campaign clears joint structural, numerical, useful-outcome, resource, safety, rights, tail, causal, reproduction, and transfer gates.
Each claimed mechanism produces its predicted signature under matched ablation without weak baselines, hidden work, censored failures, shared evaluation, or extra resources.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact mechanism, model, layer, implementation, kernel, workload, baseline, hardware, precision, budget, evaluator, rights, environment, and horizon.
Separate structure, parity, numerical behavior, parameters, operations, quality, context, stability, runtime, memory, safety, deployment, transfer, and SOTA.
Unknown kernel behavior, tail risk, fallback, recovery, reproduction, transfer, rights, or maintenance cost remains residual or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when reachable cases permit hidden winding, aliasing, collision, load concentration, numerical breach, incomplete cost, unequal baselines, broken fallback, or unowned residuals.
Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves or improves the joint frontier or weak controls, extra resources, hidden work, outcome-driven search, or shared evaluation explain it.
Refute quality, context, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA language when only structural evidence exists.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise cyclic-mechanism owner supersedes the atom and every packet, receipt, kernel, transition, incident, residual, fallback, and consumer migrates with lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/validate_circle_concrete_evidence_surface.py && python3 scripts/validate_circle_cyclic_mixer_receipt_slice.py && python3 scripts/validate_circle_multicoil_phase_receipt_slice.py && python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.CyclicMixers
reproduction.environment_lock: Local schema fixture, imported public-safe Circle structural summaries, and finite module current through 2026-07-15; natural real-kernel campaign not frozen or run.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Current validators and imported summaries are local/authored or from one Circle checkout; independent mechanism, kernel, evaluator, attack, and transfer implementations are required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and run natural workloads with actual cyclic candidates, strong matched and negative controls, real kernels and hardware, complete costs and failure denominators, causal ablations, fallback/recovery, independent implementations/evaluators, and heterogeneous transfer.

non_claims: No structural receipt or finite theorem proves trained-model quality, usable context, speed, memory, stability, efficiency, safety, deployment, transfer, support, or SOTA.
No local trained cyclic model, full baseline matrix, real kernel benchmark, independent reproduction, transfer, or chapter-core transition is represented.
No private Theseus outcome or external source-reported result is imported as ASI Stack evidence.

coilra-multicoil-rope-and-cyclic-mixers..strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary mechanisms dominate without cyclic-specific governance or empirical benefit.
coilra-multicoil-rope-and-cyclic-mixers..falsifier: Natural matched real-kernel campaigns show no unique joint advantage or fail structural, numerical, cost, fallback, reproduction, or transfer gates.

non_claims: Completing this semantic review does not promote chapter support or establish a cyclic model, kernel, quality, runtime, memory, safety, deployment, transfer, or SOTA result.
The five mappings and structural receipts do not establish downstream private or deployment behavior.
The future campaign is a required attempt specification, not a completed experiment.
